description: Read and write quorums chosen so R + W <= N let a read miss the most recent acknowledged write and return stale data as success.
body: Failure mode: an operator lowers R or W for latency so read and write sets no longer intersect, and a read served entirely by replicas that missed the latest write returns an old value with a normal success status. Detection signals: a read immediately after an acknowledged write returns the prior value; read-your-writes tests fail intermittently; N, R, and W settings show R + W <= N. Do not weaken quorum sizes to chase latency without re-deriving the guarantee. Safer fallback: restore R + W > N (and W > N/2) for the affected keyspace, add a read-your-writes regression test, and document the latency-versus-consistency tradeoff before changing it again.
